example: There was a surge in demand for face masks during the pandemic.
mnemonic: 라틴어 surgere(일어나다)에서 유래. surg(일어나다) + e = 솟아오르다 → 급증. resurge(재기하다), source(원천)와 같은 어근

example: Students are expected to maintain decorum during the ceremony.
mnemonic: decor(장식, 어울리는) + um = 어울리는 행동 → 예의. decorate(장식하다)와 같은 어근

example: It is an axiom of economics that supply affects demand.
mnemonic: 그리스어 axios(가치 있는, 마땅한)에서 유래. axi(마땅한) + om = 마땅히 옳은 것 → 공리, 자명한 이치

example: The economy gradually returned to equilibrium after the crisis.
mnemonic: '이퀼리브리엄' → 'equi(같은)+libr(저울)+ium' 저울이 같은 = 균형!

example: The hikers were in great peril when the storm hit the mountain.
mnemonic: 라틴어 periculum(시도, 위험)에서 유래. peril = 시도에 따르는 위험. perilous(위험한)의 어근

example: The discovery heralded a new era in medical science.
mnemonic: '헤럴드' → '헤럴드(Herald)' 신문 이름 = 소식을 알리는 전령!

example: His heretical views were condemned by the religious authorities.
mnemonic: heresy(이단) + tical = 이단의 → 이단적인. 그리스어 hairesis(선택)에서 유래

example: A brisk walk in the morning helps to clear the mind.
mnemonic: 고대 프랑스어 brusque(날카로운)와 관련. 날카롭게 빠른 → 활발한

example: The architect harmonized modern design with traditional elements.
mnemonic: '하모나이즈' → 'harmony(조화)+ize' 조화를 이루게 하다!

example: She showed great strength in the face of adversity.
mnemonic: ad(~쪽으로) + vers(돌다) + ity = 반대로 돌아가는 상황 → 역경. adverse(불리한)의 명사형

example: The engineers built a prototype of the new electric car.
mnemonic: proto(최초의) + type(유형) = 최초의 유형 → 원형, 시제품

example: Propaganda can have an insidious effect on public opinion.
mnemonic: in(안에) + sid(앉다) + ious = 안에 몰래 앉아있는 → 교활한. reside(거주하다)와 같은 어근

example: The research methodology was clearly described in the paper.
mnemonic: method(방법) + ology(학문) = 방법에 대한 학문 → 방법론. biology(생물학)와 같은 어근 logy

example: The spy managed to infiltrate the enemy's headquarters.
mnemonic: '인필트레이트' → 'in+filtr(여과)+ate' 스며들어 침투하다!

example: He felt a strong paternal responsibility toward his children.
mnemonic: pater(아버지) + nal = 아버지의 → 부성의. patriot(애국자), patrimony(세습 재산)와 같은 어근

example: The translation captured the original text with great fidelity.
mnemonic: fidel(믿음) + ity = 믿음직한 → 충실. confide(신뢰하다), confidence(자신감)와 같은 어근 fid(믿다)

example: The police seized millions of dollars in counterfeit currency.
mnemonic: counter(반대) + feit(만들다) = 반대로 만든 것 → 위조의. manufacture(제조)와 같은 어근 fact/feit

example: His voice was so feeble that we could barely hear him.
mnemonic: '피블' → '피(fee)+블(blow)' 바람에도 날아갈 듯 약한!

example: Her efforts to improve the community are truly laudable.
mnemonic: laud(칭찬하다) + able = 칭찬할 수 있는 → 칭찬할 만한. applaud(박수치다)와 같은 어근

example: The university has a rigorous admissions process.
mnemonic: rigor(엄격함) + ous = 엄격한. 라틴어 rigor(뻣뻣함, 엄격)에서 유래. rigid(딱딱한)와 같은 어근

example: The government used force to suppress the peaceful protests.
mnemonic: sup(아래로) + press(누르다) = 아래로 눌러 억누르다 → 억압하다. compress(압축), depress(우울하게 하다)와 같은 어근

example: Plants propagate through seeds, cuttings, or division.
mnemonic: pro(앞으로) + pag(고정하다) + ate = 앞으로 퍼뜨리며 고정하다 → 전파하다. propaganda(선전)와 같은 어근

example: Trust is a cardinal principle of any good relationship.
mnemonic: 라틴어 cardo(경첩, 축)에서 유래. cardin(핵심) + al = 문이 돌아가는 핵심 축 → 주요한, 기본적인

example: Small businesses can thrive with the right government support.
mnemonic: 고대 노르드어 thrifask(잡다, 잡아쥐다)에서 유래 → 기회를 잡아 번성하다

example: His philanthropy has improved the lives of thousands of people.
mnemonic: phil(사랑하다) + anthro(인간) + py = 인간을 사랑함 → 박애. philosophy(철학), anthropology(인류학)와 같은 어근

example: The capricious weather made it difficult to plan outdoor events.
mnemonic: capri(염소) + cious = 염소처럼 이리저리 뛰는 → 변덕스러운. Capricorn(염소자리)과 같은 어근

example: The country underwent an agrarian reform to redistribute land.
mnemonic: agr(밭) + arian = 밭에 관한 → 농업의. agriculture(농업)와 같은 어근

example: Overfishing has seriously depleted the ocean's fish populations.
mnemonic: de(완전히) + plete(채우다) = 완전히 비우다 → 고갈시키다. complete(완성하다), replete(가득 찬)와 같은 어근 ple(채우다)

example: The documentary enlightened viewers about the effects of deforestation.
mnemonic: '인라이튼' → 'en+lighten(밝히다)' 빛을 비춰 계몽하다!

example: She presented a coherent argument that convinced everyone.
mnemonic: co(함께) + her(달라붙다) + ent = 함께 붙어있는 → 일관된. adhere(접착하다), inherent(내재한)와 같은 어근

example: There is no panacea for the complex problems facing education today.
mnemonic: pan(모든) + acea(치료) = 모든 것을 치료하는 → 만병통치약. panorama(파노라마), pandemic(팬데믹)과 같은 접두사

example: He renounced his claim to the throne in favor of his brother.
mnemonic: re(뒤로) + nounce(알리다) = 알리며 뒤로 물러나다 → 포기하다. announce(발표하다), pronounce(발음하다)와 같은 어근

example: Meteorologists warned that a severe storm was imminent.
mnemonic: '이미넌트' → 'im(위에)+min(돌출)+ent' 머리 위에 닥친 = 임박한!

example: The remote village was inaccessible during the rainy season.
mnemonic: '인액세서블' → 'in(not)+accessible(접근 가능)' 접근 불가능한!

example: Japan is often described as a relatively homogeneous society.
mnemonic: '호모지니어스' → 'homo(같은)+gene(유전자)+ous' 같은 유전자의!

example: The warm weather in December was considered an anomaly.
mnemonic: a(not) + nom(규칙, 법칙) + aly = 규칙에 맞지 않는 것 → 변칙, 이례적인 것

example: New evidence bolstered the prosecution's case.
mnemonic: 고대 영어 bolster(베개, 받침)에서 유래 → 받쳐주다 → 강화하다, 북돋우다

example: Workers dismantled the old factory piece by piece.
mnemonic: dis(벗기다) + mantle(망토, 덮개) = 덮개를 벗기다 → 해체하다. mantle(망토)에서 유래

example: A good leader must show empathy toward their team members.
mnemonic: em(안에) + pathy(감정) = 안에서 느끼는 감정 → 공감. sympathy(동정), apathy(무관심)와 같은 어근 path(감정)

example: The new evidence vindicated the accused man after years in prison.
mnemonic: vindic(주장하다, 복수하다) + ate = 자신의 주장을 입증하다 → 옹호하다. revenge(복수), avenge(보복하다)와 같은 어근 계열

example: Employees receive a salary increment every year.
mnemonic: '인크리먼트' → 'in+cre(성장)+ment' 안에서 자라는 = 증가!

example: The film garnered critical acclaim and several awards.
mnemonic: '가너' → '가(gather)+너(earn)' 모아서 얻다 = 획득!

example: The company uses proprietary software that is not available to the public.
mnemonic: propri(소유) + etary = 소유에 관한 → 독점적인. property(재산), appropriate(적절한)와 같은 어근

example: Scientists extrapolated future climate trends from existing data.
mnemonic: extra(밖으로) + pol(다듬다) + ate = 기존 데이터 밖으로 확장하다 → 추론하다. interpolate(보간하다)와 같은 구조

example: The region experienced intermittent showers throughout the day.
mnemonic: inter(사이) + mitt(보내다) + ent = 사이사이에 보내는 → 간헐적인. admit(인정하다), submit(제출하다)와 같은 어근

example: The government declared a moratorium on nuclear testing.
mnemonic: mora(지체) + torium = 지체시키는 것 → 유예, 일시 중지. 라틴어 morari(지체하다)에서 유래

example: In many cultures, elderly people are venerated for their wisdom.
mnemonic: vener(사랑, 존경) + ate = 존경하다 → 숭배하다. Venus(비너스, 사랑의 여신)와 같은 어근

example: Vitamin D deficiency can lead to bone problems.
mnemonic: de(아래, 부족) + fic(만들다) + ency = 충분히 만들지 못하는 → 결핍. efficient(효율적), factory(공장)와 같은 어근 fac/fic

example: Wind and rain slowly eroded the cliff face.
mnemonic: '이로드' → 'e+rode(갉다)' 갉아서 없애다 = 침식!

example: The army deployed additional troops along the border.
mnemonic: de(풀다) + ploy(접다) = 접은 것을 펼치다 → 배치하다, 전개하다. employ(고용하다), imply(암시하다)와 같은 어근 ply/ploy
